hq: 1111B S Governors Ave, STE 37396, Dover, DE 19904

system_description.overview: Freya is an advanced Voice AI platform that automates inbound and outbound customer calls for regulated industries such as insurance, banking, fintech, and retail. The platform combines natural conversation AI with real-time compliance and security monitoring, enabling organizations to handle thousands of calls efficiently while maintaining full regulatory compliance.

products.AI Call Automation Platform: Automates inbound and outbound phone calls with human-like Voice AI; handles lead qualification, customer support, renewals, and payment reminders; integrates with CRMs, call center software (Salesforce, Daktela, Amazon Connect), and internal systems
products.Voice Intelligence & Automation Tools: AI models trained for regulated industries; real-time monitoring and error flagging; call analytics dashboard with sentiment and outcome tracking
products.Implementation & Onboarding: Engineering team handles setup, integration, and customization for each client
products.Ongoing Optimization & Support: Continuous monitoring of agent performance, voice model improvements

regions: EU and US hosting options mentioned
infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices; no physical servers; all data processed on cloud services
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (production servers hosted in high availability data centers with multiple availability zones)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: System hosted in AWS VPC environment with segmented VPCs and access control lists (ACLs); public subnet with Route 53 DNS and Application Load Balancer; private subnet with App Runner Service; network segmentation separates systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services; firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via AWS to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings; intrusion detection system utilized to monitor and detect unauthorized access or anomalies within the network
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access done through bastion host with VPN
network_architecture.tls: TLS (Transport Layer Security) using certificates from established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; all systems restricted to internet with content filtering
network_architecture.topology: Internet traffic -> Route 53 DNS resolver -> Application Load Balancer (public subnet) -> App Runner Service (private subnet) -> Auto Scaling across Multi-AZ; KMS for secrets decryption; Amazon ECR for container images; Security Groups and IAM for access control

application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments; changes tested in development or test environment before implementation

databases: Not explicitly named; production database access restricted per RBAC
data_storage.backup_frequency: Daily (backups taken daily per CC7.4.2 test evidence)

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS IAM (Active Directory, server, and database accounts referenced)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all remote access and for user accounts with administrative access to production platform; source code tool restricted to authorized users using MFA
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Passwords controlled through AWS password policy; includes periodic forced changes, password expiry, and complexity requirements; accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest (AWS KMS implied)
encryption.key_management: AWS KMS (cloud provider key management service; access to encryption keys restricted to authorized personnel)

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named (source code management tool with MFA; code repository branch rules configured)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to move change to production environment requires additional approval; developers cannot make changes to production code without additional approval
ci_cd_devops.iac: Baseline configurations retained within the infrastructure as code; configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback
ci_cd_devops.container_orchestration: AWS App Runner (container-based deployment; fetches container image from Amazon ECR)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management set up by CTO/Management; monthly patch management reviews; workstations configured to receive automatic updates; workstations scanned for patch compliance daily; OS patches for network devices handled automatically

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named (monitoring tool generates alerts when predefined thresholds are met)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled to monitor administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions at application and infrastructure level, changes to functions, security configurations, permissions, and roles; access to change log configuration is restricted
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events; change-management/ticketing application used to track logs and alerts

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure, data center services (subservice organization)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software installed and configured on all production servers (continuous scanning, weekly definition updates for servers) and on Mac-based workstations and laptops
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scanning on infrastructure and applications; scan frequency adjustable; management reviews high and critical vulnerabilities; remediation plan developed
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party; high/critical findings tracked to resolution via incident management process

untestable: CC2.2.3 - No significant changes to people/roles made during engagement period (untestable)
CC3.2.1 - Cybersecurity insurance could not be tested because no cyber incidents reported during engagement period
CC7.3.1 - Security incidents could not be tested because no security incidents reported during engagement period
CC6.5.2 - Customer data deletion upon termination could not be tested because no customer terminations during engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access controls excluded; AWS responsible for physical security of data center
A1.2 - Environmental protections excluded; Freya does not have physical office; managed by building management/AWS
Processing Integrity criteria - determined not relevant to system
Privacy criteria - determined not relevant to system
notable_controls: CC7.4.2 - Backups taken daily, multi-availability zones configured, alerts in place for backup failures
CC8.1.2 - Branch protection enforced; every merge request to production requires additional approval
CC6.2.2 - Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including administrative privileges
CC4.1.1 - Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
CC7.1.2 - Monthly automated vulnerability scanning on infrastructure and applications
Network diagram present showing VPC, public/private subnets, ALB, App Runner, ECR, KMS, IAM, Auto Scaling

infrastructure.rationale: AWS-hosted with multi-AZ, VPC, IaC (configs as code), App Runner with auto-scaling, and EU+US hosting options. No explicit multi-region failover and CDN not mentioned. Diagram shows well-structured architecture.

application_architecture.rationale: Container-based App Runner deployment with separate environments and branch protection. No programming languages or frameworks disclosed. Architecture appears modern (containerized, serverless-adjacent) but limited detail.

data_layer.rationale: Daily backups with multi-AZ confirmed. Data classification and retention policies exist. Per-tenant segregation mentioned. However, specific database technologies not named, no caching or data warehouse disclosed.

security_posture.rationale: Strong posture: IDS/IPS, firewalls, MFA enforced, monthly vuln scanning, annual external pen test, anti-malware on all servers and workstations, cybersecurity insurance, quarterly access reviews, content filtering. WAF not explicitly mentioned.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC confirmed, branch protection enforced with mandatory PR approvals, separate environments, container deployment via ECR/App Runner, automated patch management, monthly patch reviews. CI/CD tool name not disclosed.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, annually reviewed and tested per defined schedule (each scenario tested at least annually). Daily backups with multi-AZ. RTO/RPO not disclosed which limits score.

vendor_diversity.rationale: AWS is the sole named cloud provider with no secondary provider. Integration partners (Salesforce, Amazon Connect, Daktela) named. Heavy AWS concentration is a single-point-of-dependency risk.

overall.rationale: Freya demonstrates a solid security-first SOC 2 Type 2 posture with clean audit (no exceptions). Infrastructure is well-architected on AWS with good security controls. Main gaps are undisclosed specifics (DB tech, CI/CD tool, languages) and AWS-only concentration.

red_flags: Single cloud provider (AWS only) with no secondary provider or multi-region failover disclosed
RTO and RPO not disclosed in the report
yellow_flags: Programming languages and frameworks not disclosed
WAF not explicitly mentioned despite ALB being in scope
No CDN mentioned for a voice AI platform serving global clients (EU and US)
3-month audit period (Aug-Nov 2025) is relatively short for a SOC 2 Type 2
green_flags: Clean SOC 2 Type 2 audit with zero exceptions across ~79 controls
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
MFA enforced on all remote access and admin accounts
Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration confirmed via test evidence
BCDR plan annually reviewed and tested per defined schedule
Quarterly access reviews including administrative privileges
Branch protection with mandatory PR approvals enforced
Network diagram present showing clear VPC segmentation
Anti-malware on all production servers and workstations
Content filtering via proxy server for all endpoints
key_observations: Freya uses AWS App Runner (serverless container service) rather than EC2/ECS, indicating a modern, managed deployment approach with reduced operational overhead

The report covers Security only (5 trust criteria possible); Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy are not in scope — notable gap for a voice AI platform handling sensitive financial/insurance data
EU and US hosting options mentioned suggests data residency controls available for European customers, which is meaningful for financial services compliance
The CAIO role (Chief AI Officer) is explicitly in the org chart, indicating AI governance is a formal function — unusual and positive for an AI-native company
Platform integrates natively with Amazon Connect (AWS call center), creating deep AWS ecosystem lock-in beyond just infrastructure
Cybersecurity insurance was listed as a control but could not be tested operationally during the audit period (no incidents to test against)
